# Requirements Quality Checklist: Use Case Specification Expansion

**Purpose**: Validate requirement completeness, clarity, consistency, and coverage for the CMS use-case specification.
**Created**: February 5, 2026
**Feature**: `/home/ajchan/ECE493/lab/lab2/493-lab2-final/specs/001-usecase-specification/spec.md`

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are all 23 use cases represented as user stories with explicit flows? [Completeness, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]
- [x] CHK002 Are required actor roles (guest/author/reviewer/editor/attendee) explicitly stated for each relevant user story? [Completeness, Spec §User Stories]
- [x] CHK003 Are all external service interactions mentioned in use cases reflected in requirements or dependencies (auth, database, upload, payment, notification, public website, scheduling)? [Completeness, Spec §Dependencies/§Functional Requirements]
- [x] CHK004 Are time-window constraints (submission, review, registration) explicitly stated as requirements or assumptions? [Completeness, Spec §Assumptions/§FR-017/§FR-022/§FR-023]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Is “minimum required draft information” defined with specific fields? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014]
- [x] CHK006 Are accepted manuscript formats and size limits stated as concrete values? [Clarity, Spec §FR-013]
- [x] CHK007 Is “review period” unambiguously defined for both access and submission? [Clarity, Spec §Clarifications/§FR-022/§FR-023]
- [x] CHK008 Is “publicly accessible” for schedules defined without ambiguous access assumptions? [Clarity, Spec §FR-029/§User Story 20]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK009 Do login identifier statements align between user stories and clarifications? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications/§User Story 3/5]
- [x] CHK010 Do password rules align between clarifications, assumptions, and FR-003? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications/§Assumptions/§FR-003]
- [x] CHK011 Are manuscript constraints consistent across submission, update, and validation stories and FR-013? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 6-8/§FR-013]
- [x] CHK012 Are reviewer assignment constraints (exactly three reviewers, workload limit five) consistent between User Story 10 and FR-019? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 10/§FR-019]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK013 Are acceptance scenarios written in Given/When/Then with testable outcomes for each user story? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]
- [x] CHK014 Do acceptance scenarios include at least one failure condition for each user story where use cases define extensions? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]
- [x] CHK015 Are success criteria measurable and objectively verifiable? [Measurability, Spec §Success Criteria]
- [x] CHK016 Is there traceability from success criteria to specific user stories or requirements? [Traceability, Spec §Success Criteria/§Functional Requirements]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK017 Are primary flows captured for every use case, including scheduling and registration flows? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]
- [x] CHK018 Are alternate flows (extensions) represented either in acceptance scenarios or edge cases? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases/§User Scenarios & Testing]
- [x] CHK019 Are exception scenarios for external service failures (auth, upload, payment, notification) explicitly covered? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases/§Functional Requirements]
- [x] CHK020 Are recovery or resumption paths documented for drafts, failed uploads, or delayed tickets? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 7/9/22/§Edge Cases]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK021 Are boundary conditions documented for file size, reviewer limits, and reviewer counts? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases/§User Story 6/10]
- [x] CHK022 Are no-data scenarios defined (no accepted papers, no accepted assignments, missing pricing)? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK023 Are deadline-closed scenarios defined for submission and review? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases/§Assumptions]
- [x] CHK024 Are scheduling conflict scenarios defined with explicit resolution expectations? [Edge Case, Spec §User Story 19/§Edge Cases]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK025 Are any security or privacy requirements for credentials and payments explicitly stated? [Gap, Non-Functional]
- [x] CHK026 Are accessibility requirements (keyboard, contrast, screen reader) specified? [Gap, Non-Functional]
- [x] CHK027 Are reliability or availability expectations for external services specified? [Gap, Non-Functional]
- [x] CHK028 Are performance or time targets in success criteria explicitly justified by use cases or acceptance tests? [Clarity, Spec §Success Criteria]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK029 Are dependencies mapped to the specific requirements they enable? [Dependencies, Spec §Dependencies/§Functional Requirements]
- [x] CHK030 Are assumptions clearly separated from requirements and labeled as such? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK031 Do clarifications update corresponding requirements without leaving conflicting prior statements? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications/§Functional Requirements]
- [x] CHK032 Are any unvalidated assumptions flagged for follow-up before implementation? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK033 Are key terms (Draft, Submitted, Reviews Complete) defined consistently as states? [Ambiguity, Spec §User Stories/§Functional Requirements]
- [x] CHK034 Are any open issues from use cases (e.g., registration fields) explicitly resolved or flagged? [Ambiguity, Spec §User Story 1/§Assumptions]
- [x] CHK035 Are there conflicts between clarifications and original use case wording that remain unnoted? [Conflict, Spec §Clarifications/§User Stories]
- [x] CHK036 Is the requirement and acceptance criteria ID scheme consistently used without gaps or duplicates? [Traceability, Spec §Functional Requirements]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
